zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabel de Horton於1252年出生在英國諾圖波藍德布萊斯時，她的父親Walran de Horton64歲，母親Agnes de Vaux45歲。她於1256年與Thomas Castre結婚。她去世於1284年5月6日在英國諾圖波藍德霍頓享年32歲。

zh-hans.Blyth, Northumberland, England: 英国諾圖波藍德布萊斯
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Isabel de Horton于1252年出生在英国諾圖波藍德布萊斯时，她的父亲Walran de Horton64岁，母亲Agnes de Vaux45岁。她于1256年与Thomas Castre结婚。她去世于1284年5月6日在英国諾圖波藍德霍頓享年32岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Horton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Staffordshire and Warwickshire): habitational name from one or other of the many places so called, such as those in Berkshire, Buckinghamshire, Dorset, Gloucestershire, Herefordshire, Northamptonshire, Shropshire, Somerset, Staffordshire, Wiltshire, and Yorkshire. Most of the placenames derive from Old English horh or horu ‘dirt, filth’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’, though some may have different origins, including Horton in Gloucestershire, which may derive from Old English heorot ‘hart, stag’ + tūn.

variants.name: Horten
meanings: Norwegian: habitational name from any of several farms named Horten, from an unattested word denoting ‘uneven, hard and lumpy soil’.

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Isabel de Horton was born in 1252, in Blyth, Northumberland, England, her father, Walran de Horton, was 64 and her mother, Agnes de Vaux, was 45. She married Thomas Castre in 1256. She died on 6 May 1284, in Horton, Northumberland, England, at the age of 32.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Isabel de Horton est née en 1252, à Blyth, Northumberland, Angleterre, son père, Walran de Horton, avait 64 ans et sa mère, Agnes de Vaux, avait 45 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas Castre en 1256. Elle est décédée le 6 mai 1284, à Horton, Northumberland, Angleterre, à l’âge de 32 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Isabel de Horton 1252 in Blyth, Northumberland, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Walran de Horton 64 und ihre Mutter Agnes de Vaux 45. Sie hat 1256 Thomas Castre geheiratet. Sie starb am 6. Mai 1284 im Alter von 32 Jahren in Horton, Northumberland, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel de Horton è nata il nel 1252 nel Blyth, Northumberland, England, sua padre, Walran de Horton, aveva 64 anni e sua madre, Agnes de Vaux, aveva 45 anni. Lei sposato Thomas Castre nel 1256. Morì il 6 maggio 1284, nel Horton, Northumberland, England, all'età di 32 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Isabel de Hortonが1252年にイギリスノースウンバーランドブリスで生まれた時，父Walran de Hortonは64歳，母Agnes de Vauxは45歳であった。1256年にThomas Castreと結婚した。彼女は1284年5月6日にイギリスノースウンバーランドホールトンで32歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Isabel de Horton(이)가 1252년 영국 노르스벌랜드 블리스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Walran de Horton(은)는 64세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Agnes de Vaux(은)는 45세였다. 그녀는 1256년 Thomas Castre(와)과 결혼했다. 그녀는 1284년 5월 6일 영국 노르스벌랜드 호르턴에서 32세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel de Horton nasceu em 1252, em Blyth, Northumberland, Inglaterra, seu pai, Walran de Horton, tinha 64 anos e sua mãe, Agnes de Vaux, tinha 45 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas Castre em 1256. Ela faleceu em 6 de maio de 1284, em Horton, Northumberland, Inglaterra, com 32 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Isabel de Horton родилась в 1252 г. в местности блит, нортумбирланд, Англия, её отцу по имени Walran de Horton было 64 года, а матери, которую звали Agnes de Vaux, 45. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Castre в 1256 г. Она умерла 6 мая 1284 г. в местности хауртон, нортумбирланд, Англия в возрасте 32 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Isabel de Horton nació en 1252, en Blyth, Northumberland, Inglaterra, su papá, Walran de Horton, tenía 64 años y su mamá, Agnes de Vaux, tenía 45 años. Se casó con Thomas Castre en 1256. Murió el 6 de mayo de 1284, en Horton, Northumberland, Inglaterra, a la edad de 32 años.
